default.children: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.

variants.children: Design is not just what it looks like and feels like. Design is how it works.
In the middle of difficulty lies opportunity.
Any fool can write code that a computer can understand. Good programmers write code that humans can understand.
variants.source: Refactoring: Improving the Design of Existing Code

textOnly.children: A standalone quote with no attribution or source.

withAttribution.children: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood.

withSource.children: Any fool can write code that a computer can understand.

longText.children: The measure of intelligence is the ability to change. The measure of wisdom is the ability to know when and how to adapt to circumstances that are continually shifting beneath your feet. Understanding this fundamental truth is what separates those who succeed from those who merely survive in an ever-changing world.
longText.attribution: A Very Long Author Name That Demonstrates Truncation Behaviour

noAttribution.children: A quote without attribution or source rendered as a bare blockquote.
